sourceNote: Audited code: the Order workflow of github.com/temporalio/reference-app-orders-go (app/order/workflows.go, vendored under source/). The observable state is the workflow's own StatusQuery projection: the order status plus the per-fulfillment status list.

stateKeys.name: fulfillments
stateKeys.type: array of enum: 'pending' | 'unavailable' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'cancelled' | 'failed' — one entry per fulfillment, [] before reservation

dataFields.available: array of boolean — per-fulfillment availability from the ReserveItems activity. Builds the fulfillment list. If ANY entry is false the order requires customer action; otherwise it proceeds directly to processing, at which point every fulfillment starts processing (charges are scheduled).

dataFields.action: enum: 'amend' | 'cancel'. amend: unavailable fulfillments become cancelled and the order proceeds to processing (remaining fulfillments start processing). cancel: the order becomes cancelled; fulfillment statuses are LEFT AS THEY ARE (the code does not cancel them on this path).

dataFields.index: integer — which fulfillment's Charge activity resolved (0-based)
dataFields.success: boolean — the charge outcome. success: the fulfillment stays 'processing' (its shipment child is started; no observable change). failure: the fulfillment becomes 'failed' and no shipment is started.

dataFields.index: integer — which fulfillment's Shipment child workflow completed (0-based)
dataFields.ok: boolean — ok: the fulfillment becomes 'completed'; not ok: 'failed'. When the LAST fulfillment finishes (completed/failed/cancelled all count as finished), the order becomes 'failed' if EVERY fulfillment is 'failed' (at least one), otherwise 'completed'.

specialRules.name: reservation-splits-and-routes
specialRules.note: RESERVED (only from 'pending') creates one fulfillment per availability entry: true → 'pending', false → 'unavailable'. If any is 'unavailable' → status 'customerActionRequired'. Otherwise → status 'processing' AND every fulfillment moves to 'processing' in the same step (the workflow starts all fulfillment goroutines immediately).

specialRules.name: amend-cancels-unavailable-then-processes
specialRules.note: CUSTOMER_ACTION{amend} (only from 'customerActionRequired'): every 'unavailable' fulfillment becomes 'cancelled'; status → 'processing'; every remaining 'pending' fulfillment becomes 'processing' in the same step. NOTE: if ALL fulfillments were unavailable, they all become 'cancelled' and processing completes immediately with status 'completed' IN THE SAME STEP (cancelled fulfillments are skipped, zero failures ≠ all-failed).

specialRules.name: cancel-leaves-fulfillments
specialRules.note: CUSTOMER_ACTION{cancel} (only from 'customerActionRequired'): status → 'cancelled'. Fulfillment statuses are NOT changed (they remain 'pending'/'unavailable').

specialRules.name: timeout-cancels-everything
specialRules.note: CUSTOMER_TIMEOUT (only from 'customerActionRequired'): every fulfillment becomes 'cancelled'; status → 'timedOut'.

specialRules.name: completion-rollup
specialRules.note: When a CHARGE_RESULT{success:false} or SHIPMENT_RESULT finishes the LAST unfinished fulfillment (unfinished = 'pending' or 'processing'), the order status resolves in the same step: 'failed' if every fulfillment is 'failed' and there is at least one, else 'completed'. A mix of completed/failed/cancelled — or even ALL-cancelled — yields 'completed'.

specialRules.name: out-of-scope-actions-are-noops
specialRules.note: An action arriving in a state that does not await it (e.g. CHARGE_RESULT while 'pending', CUSTOMER_ACTION while 'processing', anything in a terminal state, or an index with no matching unfinished fulfillment) yields post == pre.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply in the current state yields post == pre.
